name: Michael Strane
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary geology • Geomorphologic mapping • Surface process evolution
bio: Michael David Strane is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, where he also serves as a Graduate Teaching Assistant. His research focuses on planetary science and geology, with interests spanning astrogeology, Quaternary geology, and tectonic processes. He has experience in geomorphological and geological mapping, studying surface processes and landscape evolution to better understand planetary environments. In addition to his research, Strane contributes to undergraduate education by supporting courses such as Introduction to Observational Astronomy, reflecting his role in both academic research and teaching within the department.
